issue_title: cache-embedded-mtls-enabled maps to a build time property, but is a runtime option
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

cache-embedded-mtls-enabled maps to kc.spi-jgroups-mtls--default--enabled which is a build time option for enabling / disabling a provider.

However the option is marked as runtime. This can lead to confusing errors if we make changes to how we expose runtime options to quarkus such as https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/23953775981/job/69867750818?pr=47748#step:5:1852

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The option looks like it is supposed to be a runtime configuration, so it should not overlap with a build time option.

### Actual behavior

The option is effectively both build time and runtime.

### How to Reproduce?

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/23953775981/job/69867750818?pr=47748#step:5:1852

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
fix: mapping mtls enabled to a runtime option (#47751)

closes: #47750

Signed-off-by: Steve Hawkins <shawkins@redhat.com>

issue_title: False session type of access token in offline_access refresh token flow with scope parameter without offline_access scope
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

[Issue 37694](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/37694) correct session type of access token of other oidc/oauth flows.

However, this way could produce wrong session type of access token in offline_access refresh token flow and accordingly problems using returned access token.
Fe In offline_access refresh token flow with scope parameter without offline_access including in scope, return on session type instead of off. This access token could not be used in other flows if online session does not exist or will expired based on online sessionif it exists.

In refresh token flow, session type must be based on user session type.

### Version

26.5.6

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

1. Log into a client with `Use refresh tokens` enabled and without `offline_access` scope
2. Log into the same window in the same client with `offline_access` scope. An offline refresh token is returned.
3. Execute refresh token flow with the offline refresh token with scope parameter without `offline_access` scope.
4. Access token (token A) with session type off returned
5. Wait until online session expired or logout the client
6. Execute UserInfo with token A --> return userinfo successfully

### Actual behavior

1. Log into a client with `Use refresh tokens` enabled and without `offline_access` scope
2. Log into the same window in the same client with `offline_access` scope. An offline refresh token is returned.
3. Execute refresh token flow with the offline refresh token with scope parameter without `offline_access` scope.
4. Access token (token A) with session type on returned
5. Wait until online session expired or logout the client
6. Execute UserInfo with token A --> 401 (expired access token)

### How to Reproduce?

See actual behavior

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix session type of access token in offline access refresh token flow

Closes #47776

Signed-off-by: cgeorgilakis-grnet <cgeorgilakis@admin.grnet.gr>

issue_title: az vm create fails with JSON parsing error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ci

### Describe the bug

AzureDB IT job fails with `Extra data: line 1 column 4 (char 3)` from `az vm create --no-wait`.
Most likely caused by Azure backend change triggering a bug in `azure-core` (1.38.0+) continuation token handling.
Workaround: downgrade azure-cli to 2.82.0 (uses `azure-core` 1.37.0).

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/pull/44574
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/pull/44658

upstream/main

AzureDB IT job works.

### Actual behavior

`Extra data: line 1 column 4 (char 3)`
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/24077604334/job/70239413852

### How to Reproduce?

Run Azure workflow.

### Anything else?

issue_title: Configuration reset isn't always complete
issue_body:
### Description

I noticed there was an inconsistency because of running our quarkus unit tests in the ide and getting a failure on a health check test.

Configuration.reset should always call KeycloakConfigSourceProvider.reload() because otherwise the existing config sources will get reused and it's possible they contain stale state.
commit_message:
fix: consistently resetting the configuration (#47870)

closes: #47869

Signed-off-by: Steve Hawkins <shawkins@redhat.com>

issue_title: Not possible to sync latest keycloak-admin-client to keycloak-client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/client-java

### Describe the bug

It is not possible to sync latest keycloak-admin-client-tests to keycloak-client repository based on the instructions from https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-client/blob/main/README.md#syncing-the-files-from-keycloak-repository .

```
	at org.keycloak.exportimport.singlefile.SingleFileImportProvider.importModel(SingleFileImportProvider.java:69)
	at org.keycloak.exportimport.ExportImportManager.lambda$runImport$1(ExportImportManager.java:105)
	at java.base/java.lang.Iterable.forEach(Iterable.java:75)
```


### How to Reproduce?

- Start a Keycloak 26.6.0 instance.
- Create a realm with Admin Permissions enabled.
- Create a user resource permission with a client based policy (eg. account-console).
- Export the realm to a JSON file via the export command.
- Start a new Keycloak 26.6.0 instance with the --import-realm flag, placing the exported JSON file in the data/import directory.
- Observe the server failing to start with the error ModelValidationException: Resource [Users] does not exist for type [Users].

### Anything else?

I believe the root cause is that `AdminPermissionsSchema.getOrCreateResource()` unconditionally calls `.orElseThrow()` when `resolveUser()` (or the other resolve methods) returns an empty . 

**26.5.7**
```
String name;
switch (resourceType) {
        case CLIENTS_RESOURCE_TYPE -> name = resolveClient(session, id).map(ClientModel::getId).orElse(resourceType);
        case GROUPS_RESOURCE_TYPE -> name = resolveGroup(session, id).map(GroupModel::getId).orElse(resourceType);
        case ROLES_RESOURCE_TYPE -> name = resolveRole(session, id).map(RoleModel::getId).orElse(resourceType);
        case USERS_RESOURCE_TYPE -> name = resolveUser(session, id).map(UserModel::getId).orElse(resourceType);
        default -> throw new IllegalStateException("Resource type [" + resourceType + "] not found.");
}
resource = resourceStore.findByName(resourceServer, name);
```

**26.6.0**
```
String name;
switch (resourceType) {
        case CLIENTS_RESOURCE_TYPE -> name = resolveClient(session, id).map(ClientModel::getId).orElseThrow(() -> new ModelValidationException("Resource [" + id + "] does not exist for type [" + resourceType + "]"));
        case GROUPS_RESOURCE_TYPE -> name = resolveGroup(session, id).map(GroupModel::getId).orElseThrow(() -> new ModelValidationException("Resource [" + id + "] does not exist for type [" + resourceType + "]"));
        case ROLES_RESOURCE_TYPE -> name = resolveRole(session, id).map(RoleModel::getId).orElseThrow(() -> new ModelValidationException("Resource [" + id + "] does not exist for type [" + resourceType + "]"));
        case USERS_RESOURCE_TYPE -> name = resolveUser(session, id).map(UserModel::getId).orElseThrow(() -> new ModelValidationException("Resource [" + id + "] does not exist for type [" + resourceType + "]"));
        default -> throw new IllegalStateException("Resource type [" + resourceType + "] not found.");
}
resource = resourceStore.findByName(resourceServer, name);
```

issue_title: MigrateTo26_6_0 modifies custom browser flows, breaking existing realm authentication
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

The MigrateTo26_6_0 migration unconditionally adds an "Organization" authentication flow step to the realm's currently configured browser flow - including custom (non-built-in) flows. This breaks existing realms that use custom browser flows by injecting unexpected steps with hardcoded priorities and builtIn=true sub-flows into user-managed flow configurations.
Root Cause                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
  In MigrateTo26_6_0.java:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
  public void migrateRealm(KeycloakSession session, RealmModel realm) {                                                                                                                                                                                    
      DefaultAuthenticationFlows.addOrganizationBrowserFlowStep(realm, realm.getBrowserFlow());                                                                                                                                                            
  }                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
  realm.getBrowserFlow() returns whatever flow is currently bound as the browser flow for the realm. If a realm uses a custom browser flow (a copy of the built-in flow, with builtIn=false), the migration modifies that custom flow.                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  Problems                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  1. Custom flows are modified without consent                                                                                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  Custom flows exist precisely because users need control over their authentication steps. The migration injects new steps into these user-managed flows, which can break carefully tuned authentication logic.                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  2. builtIn=true sub-flows injected into non-built-in flows                                                                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  addOrganizationBrowserFlowStep() (DefaultAuthenticationFlows.java:837) creates the "Organization" sub-flow with builtIn=true. This creates a hybrid state: the parent flow is custom (modifiable), but the injected child is built-in (protected). Users 
  cannot reorder, modify, or remove the injected organization step through the Admin Console or REST API because:                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
  // AuthenticationManagementResource.java                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  if (parentFlow.isBuiltIn()) {                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
      throw new BadRequestException("It is illegal to modify execution in a built in flow");                                                                                                                                                               
  }

### Version

26.6.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The migration should only add the organization step to the built-in browser flow (looked up by alias "browser"), not to custom flows bound as the realm's browser flow. Users who want organization support in their custom flows should add it manually.

### Actual behavior

current MigrateTo26_6_0 manipulates existing binded custom browser flows on realm when the org authenticators are not yet present in the flow

### How to Reproduce?

1. On Keycloak < 26.6.0, create a realm                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
2. Copy the built-in "browser" flow to a custom flow (e.g., "my-browser-flow")                                                                                                                                                                         
3. Customize the flow (modify steps, reorder priorities, add custom authenticators)                                                                                                                                                                      
4. Bind "my-browser-flow" as the realm's browser flow                                                                                                                                                                                                    
5. Upgrade to Keycloak 26.6.0                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
6. Observe that "my-browser-flow" now contains an injected "Organization" sub-flow at priority 26 with builtIn=true                                                                                                                                      
7. Attempt to remove or reorder the injected step — this may fail depending on the parent flow's structure 

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: FreeMarker templates allow instantiation of new objects and even running OS commands
issue_body:
### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

The default FreeMarker configuration allows the `?new()` built-in to
instantiate arbitrary Java classes, including
`freemarker.template.utility.Execute`, which runs shell commands. 

This was reported to the Keycloak security team by Kelvin Mbogo. This was later classified as a hardening issue, and not a CVE. Due to that, it is handled publicly.

Changing Freemarker template file is something only trusted users should be allowed to do, similar to changing any other file (like JavaScript sources or adding extensions). 

### Version

26.2.0 and later

### Expected behavior

The functionality to instantiate new instances is not used by Keycloak's original templates, and therefore it should be disabled.

### Actual behavior

It is enabled. 

### How to Reproduce?

See internal issue.

### Anything else?

Suggested fix: One line added to DefaultFreeMarkerProvider.java after the Configuration is
constructed:

configuration.setNewBuiltinClassResolver(TemplateClassResolver.ALLOWS_NOTHING_RESOLVER);

This tells FreeMarker to reject all ?new() calls in templates, which is the
right default for a server-side template engine processing potentially
untrusted theme content.

Alternatively, SAFER_RESOLVER could be used if any legitimate theme needs
to instantiate specific approved classes, but ALLOWS_NOTHING_RESOLVER is
the safer default.

The documentation should be updated to clarify that Freemarker templates should only be changed by trusted users.  

---
*This issue was originally tracked in the private repository. Migrated by @ahus1.*
commit_message:
Disable FreeMarker ?new() built-in in themes. Closes #47915 (#48280)

Signed-off-by: Yike Gao <yikegao8@gmail.com>

issue_title: account-console client can be deleted despite being a built-in internal client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

The account-console client is a built-in client created automatically during realm setup, but unlike the other built-in clients (account, admin-cli, broker, realm-management, security-admin-console), it is not protected from deletion. Deleting it breaks the account management UI for end users.

### Version

main (nightly)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Attempting to delete the account-console client via the Admin REST API or Admin Console should be blocked with a 400 Bad Request error, the same as deleting any other built-in client (e.g., account, security-admin-console).

### Actual behavior

The account-console client can be successfully deleted via DELETE /admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{id} or through the Admin Console UI, breaking the account management UI for end users.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Start Keycloak and log in to the Admin Console
2. Navigate to Clients
3. Open the account-console client
4. Delete the client (via the three-dot menu or REST API)
5. The deletion succeeds
6. Compare: repeat with account or security-admin-console - the deletion is blocked with "Could not delete client"

### Anything else?

Additionally, there appears to be a broader inconsistency between the Admin Console UI and the backend regarding which clients are considered internal. The UI hides the delete button only for broker and realm-management, while the backend blocks deletion for all 5 built-in clients listed above. This means for admin-cli, account, and security-admin-console, the delete button is visible in the UI but clicking it results in an error. Should this UI/backend mismatch also be addressed as part of this issue, or tracked separately?

## Video

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/762229ac-f4eb-486b-9dd1-bf0f9dd78041

issue_title: User profile multiselect options not highlighted as selected in dropdown
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The `SelectComponent` used for user profile attributes with `inputType: multiselect` annotation does not correctly highlight selected options in the dropdown.

The `convertOptions` function in `SelectComponent.tsx` accepts `selected` as `string`, but for multiselect fields `field.value` is a `string[]`. The comparison `selected === option` always evaluates to `false` for arrays, so no options appear as selected.

### Version

latest

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When a user profile attribute is configured with `inputType: multiselect`, selected options should appear highlighted in the dropdown.

### Actual behavior

No options are visually marked as selected in the dropdown, even after selecting them. The values are saved correctly, but there is no visual feedback.

### How to Reproduce?

  1. Go to Realm Settings > User Profile > Attributes
  2. Create or edit an attribute
  3. Add a validator: `options` with values e.g. ["a", "b", "c"]
  4. Set the attribute as `multivalued`
  5. Add annotation: `inputType: multiselect`
  6. Go to Users > create/edit a user
  7. Open the multiselect dropdown for that attribute
  8. Select multiple values they are not highlighted as selected

### Anything else?

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/84135fe2-1d6b-4d78-992d-0fe557b470a5

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix multiselect options not highlighted as selected in user profile

The convertOptions function in SelectComponent accepted only a string
parameter, but for multiselect fields field.value is a string array.
This caused the selected comparison to always return false, so no
options appeared as selected in the dropdown.

Closes #47929

Signed-off-by: erenkan <eren@keymate.io>

issue_title: [CVE-2026-4628] Improper Access Control on Keycloak Server through UMA resource management endpoints via PUT parameters
issue_body:
## Description

When a resource server is configured with `allowRemoteResourceManagement=false`, Keycloak correctly blocks POST and DELETE operations on UMA resource management endpoints. However, the PUT endpoint at `/realms/{realm}/authz/protection/resource_set/{id}` does not check this configuration setting, allowing resource updates to proceed even when remote management is disabled.

This means that users with the `uma_protection` role can still modify existing UMA resources through PUT requests, even though the administrator has disabled remote resource management for that resource server.

**Prerequisites:**
- User must have the `uma_protection` role
- Resource server must have `allowRemoteResourceManagement` set to false

## Version Affected

26.5.3 and potentially other versions

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a resource server in Keycloak
2. Set `allowRemoteResourceManagement=false` on the resource server
3. Create a user with the `uma_protection` role
4. Attempt POST to `/realms/{realm}/authz/protection/resource_set` - correctly blocked
5. Attempt DELETE to `/realms/{realm}/authz/protection/resource_set/{id}` - correctly blocked
6. Attempt PUT to `/realms/{realm}/authz/protection/resource_set/{id}` with updated resource data
7. Observe that the PUT request succeeds and the resource is updated

## Expected Behavior

The PUT endpoint should check the `allowRemoteResourceManagement` setting and block update operations when remote management is disabled, consistent with POST and DELETE endpoints.

## Actual Behavior

The PUT endpoint allows resource updates regardless of the `allowRemoteResourceManagement` setting, bypassing the administrator's configuration.

## Additional Context

This issue has been assigned CVE-2026-4628 for tracking purposes.

The missing check is in `org.keycloak.authorization.protection.resource.ResourceService#update` which should call `checkResourceServerSettings` like the other endpoints do.

---
*This issue was originally tracked in the private repository. Migrated by @ahus1.*
commit_message:
Enforce the remote management check (#48111)

Closes #47932

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: IdentityProviderAuthenticator creates an infinite redirect loop when an IdP returns an error (e.g. access_denied) and the login was initiated with kc_idp_hint
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

PR #47244 (commit 3e3191d) changed IdentityProviderAuthenticator.authenticate() to read kc_idp_hint from the authentication session client note as a fallback (for PAR support). However, when the IdP callback returns an error and Keycloak re-enters the browser flow via IdentityBrokerService.browserAuthentication(), the client note still contains the original kc_idp_hint value. The authenticator picks it up and re-redirects to the same IdP, creating an infinite loop.                            
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
The existing forwardedErrorMessage guard that prevents this loop exists only in the default-provider branch — the kc_idp_hint branch has no such check.

### Version

26.6.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When an IdP returns an error (e.g. error=access_denied) during a kc_idp_hint-initiated login, IdentityProviderAuthenticator should call context.attempted() (as it did in 26.5.4), allowing subsequent authenticators in the flow to handle the error - such as showing the login page or redirecting the user back to the RP with an error.

### Actual behavior

IdentityProviderAuthenticator reads the persisted kc_idp_hint from the authentication session client note and calls redirect() → context.forceChallenge(), sending the user back to the same failing IdP. This creates an infinite redirect loop.        
Subsequent authenticators in the browser flow never execute.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure a browser flow with these ALTERNATIVE executions in order:                                                                                                                                                                                  
    - identity-provider-redirector                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
    - auth-username-password-form (or any other authenticator)                                                                                                                                                                                             
  2. Configure an external IdP (e.g. idp) in the realm                                                                                                                                                                                                
  3. Initiate a login with kc_idp_hint=idp: /realms/{realm}/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=...&kc_idp_hint=idp                                                                                                                           
  4. On the IdP side, return an error: error=access_denied&error_description=User+denied+consent                                                                                                                                                           
  5. Observe that Keycloak redirects back to the IdP instead of falling through to the next authenticator

### Anything else?

_No response_

Continue with next step when IdP returns an error

Closes #47955

issue_title: Generated openapi.yaml drifts on incremental builds
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Incremental builds (without `clean`) produce a dirty `openapi.yaml` - duplicate `bearer-auth` security entries and reordered fields. Clean builds are not affected.

I think that this is happening because SmallRye picks up the previously generated `target/classes/META-INF/openapi.yaml` as a static file and merges it back in. The merge causes reordering and `OASModelFilter.addSecurityScheme()` appends another `bearer-auth` entry on top of the merged one.

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Incremental builds should produce the same output as a clean build.

### Actual behavior

Each incremental build appends another `bearer-auth: []` and reorders YAML fields.

This can potentially cause that the changed spec will end up committed inan unrelated PR by mistake.

### How to Reproduce?

```
mvn clean process-classes -pl rest/admin-v2/rest -am   # 1st build OK
mvn process-classes -pl rest/admin-v2/rest -am          # 2nd build duplicates appear
git diff -- js/libs/keycloak-admin-client/openapi.yaml
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Prevent duplicate entries and reordering in openapi.yaml on incremental builds (#47968)

Closes: #47967

issue_title: JDBC_PING2 will stall when in a two-node-split-brain one node dies
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

The current split-brain detection looks at the database and the coordinator with the smaller address will win, and the other node will mark itself not ready. 

If that is a single node, and that single node dies, the second node will not take over the load, and will remain not ready.

### Version

26.6.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When one node dies (even in split brain), the remaining node should eventually take over.

### Actual behavior

In a split brain, when the node with lower number unexpectedly dies, the remaining node will continue to be not ready.

This is only resolved by starting one more node, or restarting the remaining node.

### How to Reproduce?

Review the code.

### Anything else?

The current implementation misses a stateless of entries. The current setup with MERGE3 will update the database regularly to keep entries alive. At least one more column in the database is needed for that.
commit_message:
Adding staleness concept to JDBC_PING

Closes #47970

Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <alexander.schwartz@ibm.com>

issue_title: [OID4VCI] credential_offer_uri can only be consumed once
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

When testing the current OID4VCI implementation of Keycloak against the german wallet from SPRIND, i found that Keycloak currently treats `credential_offer_uri` as a single use object which can only be fetched once.
That is a problem, because the Wallet splits the flow into two parts:
1. Read the Offer and VCI-Metadata from Keycloak, then present a screen to the user with all information to accept the offer or decline it
2.  Fetch the offer again to actually import the credential

Step 2 right now fails with: 
`{"error":"invalid_credential_offer_request","error_description":"Credential offer not found or already consumed"}`

As far as i can tell, the spec doesnt require the offer_uri to be one-time only, so changing it would improve interoperability.

### Version

26.6.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Let the wallet fetch the offer multiple times

### Actual behavior

Offer can only be fetched once

### How to Reproduce?

Call the same credential_offer_uri multiple times

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: User session limit generates fatal error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

_No response_

### Describe the bug

UserSessionLimitsAuthenticator implicitly assumes that whenever the realm session limit is exceeded, all client session limits are  also exceeded.

Introduced by #38016

### Version

26.5.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

UserSessionLimitsAuthenticator should correctly handle cases where a user exceeds the realm session limit without exceeding all client session limits, and must do so without causing a runtime error.

As a possible solution, when the realm limit is exceeded but some client limits are not, the system could purge the oldest user sessions across all clients, rather than restricting the cleanup to sessions of the current client.

### Actual behavior

When a user exceeds the realm session limit but has not exceeded the session limit for a specific client, attempting to log in to that client results in a runtime error.

 

### How to Reproduce?

Steps to reproduce:

1. Create a realm with two clients, A and B.
2. Configure an authentication flow with identical session limits applied at both the realm and client levels.
3. Log in repeatedly to client A until both the realm session limit and client A’s session limit are exceeded for a specific user.
4. Attempt to log in to client B using the same user.

Actual result:
The login attempt fails with an internal server error.

### Anything else?

Relevant code (UserSessionLimitsAuthenticator.java)

```
            if (newUserSession && exceedsLimit(userSessionCountForRealm, userRealmLimit)) {
                logger.infof("Too many session in this realm for the current user. Session count: %s", userSessionCountForRealm);
                String eventDetails = String.format("Realm session limit exceeded. Realm: %s, Realm limit: %s. Session count: %s, User id: %s",
                        context.getRealm().getName(), userRealmLimit, userSessionCountForRealm, context.getUser().getId());
                var removedClientSessions = handleLimitExceeded(context, userSessionsForClient, eventDetails, userClientLimit);
```

When the realm limit is exceeded, the implementation attempts to purge sessions based on the current client’s limit first.

```
    private long getNumberOfSessionsThatNeedToBeLoggedOut(long count, long limit) {
        return count - (limit - 1);
    }
```

This method is used to determine how many sessions should be removed for the current client. However, it returns a negative value when the client limit has not been exceeded.

```
       List<UserSessionModel> userSessionsToBeRemoved = userSessions
            .stream()
            .sorted(Comparator.comparingInt(UserSessionModel::getLastSessionRefresh))
            .limit(numberOfSessionsThatNeedToBeLoggedOut)
            .toList();
```

A negative number as argument to limit() produces an runtime exception which leads to the internal server error.
commit_message:
when the realm session limit is exceeded and behavior is set to Termi… (#48064)

* when the realm session limit is exceeded and behavior is set to Terminate oldest session, the authenticator calls
handleLimitExceeded(userSessionsForClient, userClientLimit) first.

if the user has fewer existing sessions for the current client than userClientLimit - 1, getNumberOfSessionsThatNeedToBeLoggedOut() returns a negative value and Stream.limit(negative) throws
IllegalArgumentException, producing a 500 on every login to that client.

This change ensures that getNumberOfSessionsThatNeedToBeLoggedOut returns 0 if the calculated value is negative.
The dependent execution steps remain unchanged.
A result of 0 sessions to be purged from the current client triggers the removal of the oldest sessions across all clients.

also added a regression test in UserSessionLimitsTest that reproduces the scenario: realm limit=2, client limit=3, two logins on client-a to saturate the realm, then login on client-b (0 existing client sessions).

Fixes #48040

---------

Signed-off-by: Martin Krcka <martin.krcka@adesso.de>
Signed-off-by: Martin Bartoš <mabartos@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Mateen Anjum <mateenali66@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Martin Bartoš <mabartos@redhat.com>

issue_title: OID4VC QR Code Unbounded Dimensions DoS
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

File: OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.java lines 435-436, 536-539
 
width and height query parameters have no upper bound:

``` 
    @QueryParam("width") https://github.com/DefaultValue("200") int width,
    @QueryParam("height") https://github.com/DefaultValue("200") int height
``` 
 

### Acknowledgement

This was shared with the Keycloak security team by @Medoedus. This is handled as a public issue as this OID4VC is still an experimental feature.


### Version

26.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

System should not OOM / should validate the parameters

### Actual behavior

Passed directly to ZXing QRCodeWriter which allocates BitMatrix of width*height bits. Request with width=50000&height=50000 allocates ~2.5GB.


### How to Reproduce?

 Reachability: `GET /realms/{realm}/protocol/oid4vc/create-credential-offer?type=qr&width=50000&height=50000` with valid bearer token.

### Anything else?

Fix: Add MAX_QR_DIMENSION constant (e.g. 2000) and validate before generation.

issue_title: [CVE-2026-37980] Stored XSS in select-organization.ftl - FreeMarker HTML-escape insufficient in inline JS handler
issue_body:
## Description

A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) issue exists in the organization selection login page (`select-organization.ftl`). When an organization alias contains malicious JavaScript code, FreeMarker's `HTMLOutputFormat` escapes the value for HTML attribute context, but this escaping is insufficient when the attribute is an inline JavaScript `onclick` handler.

The browser HTML-decodes entities before passing the value to the JavaScript engine, allowing the injected script to execute. This occurs because the template uses `${organization.alias}` directly in an `onclick` attribute without JavaScript-specific escaping.

**Prerequisites:**
- User must have `manage-realm` or `manage-organizations` permission to create organizations with malicious aliases
- Organizations feature must be enabled in the realm

## Version Affected

26.5.5 and potentially other versions

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Log in as a user with `manage-realm` or `manage-organizations` permission
2. Create a new organization with an alias containing JavaScript code
3. Navigate to the login page where organization selection is displayed
4. Click on the organization entry
5. Observe that the JavaScript code executes in the browser

## Expected Behavior

Organization aliases should be properly escaped for JavaScript context when used in inline event handlers. The system should either:
- Use JavaScript-specific escaping for values in `onclick` handlers
- Avoid inline event handlers entirely and use event listeners instead
- Validate organization aliases to prevent JavaScript-unsafe characters

## Actual Behavior

The organization alias is only HTML-escaped, which is insufficient for inline JavaScript context. The browser decodes HTML entities before executing the JavaScript, allowing the injected code to run.

## Additional Context

This issue has been assigned CVE-2026-37980 for tracking purposes.

While this requires elevated administrative permissions (`manage-realm` or `manage-organizations`), it could be problematic in scenarios where:
- Organization names are provisioned automatically via API/automation that assumes all characters are safe
- A compromised admin account could use this to escalate access to other realms
- Multiple administrators with different trust levels manage organizations

The team is planning a comprehensive review of FreeMarker templates to identify similar context-specific escaping issues.

---
*This issue was originally tracked in the private repository. Migrated by @ahus1.*

issue_title: When a group is granted a role, it should not read all existing role mappings from the database
issue_body:
### Description

`GroupAdapter.hasDirectRole()` falls back to the default `RoleMapperModel` implementation, which calls `getRoleMappingsStream()` - loading all role mappings from the database (and potentially hitting the cache/realm lookup) just to check if a single mapping exists. This is called from `grantRole()` as a guard check.

### Value Proposition

Faster and near-constant time when granting a role to a group. Replaces O(n) stream scan with a single targeted named query (`groupHasRole`).                                                                    


### Goals

Override `hasDirectRole()` in `GroupAdapter` to use the existing `groupHasRole` named query directly, matching the optimization already done in `UserAdapter`.

### Non-Goals

Analyzing similar problems in other adapters.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Notes

Same pattern as the `UserAdapter` [optimization](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/45689). Submitting as a pull request.
commit_message:
Override hasDirectRole in GroupAdapter to use targeted query

Closes #48088

issue_title: Clarify canonicalization in X.509 authentication
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The [X.509 authentication documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_x509-config) explains the *Canonical DN representation enabled* option. This options uses the [canonical](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/javax/security/auth/x500/X500Principal.html#getName-java.lang.String-) format defined in the JDK. This format performs some modifications (for example uppercase/lowercase) to the DN that can modify the exact UTF-8 string in the certificate. We need to add a note/warning to clarify/notice this behavior.

### Version

26.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Just add a note to clarify the fact the DN is changed and can trigger issues with locales.

### Actual behavior

No clarification note.

### How to Reproduce?

N/A

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add info note to the X.509 Canonical DN representation enabled option

Closes #48123
